params.amount: Number of outcome tokens to buy (1e18 = 1 token = 1 G$)
params.isYES: True for YES tokens, false for NO tokens

params.question: Human-readable question (e.g., "Will BTC exceed $100k by 2026?")
params.resolver: Address authorised to resolve this market (admin if zero)

params.amount: Number of winning tokens to redeem

setMarketCreator(address,bool).details: Idempotent: setting an already-true value to true (or already-false      to false) is a no-op other than re-emitting the event. The admin      itself does not need to be added — it is always authorised.

params.allowed: True to grant, false to revoke.
params.who: Address whose market-creator status to update.

voidMarket(uint256).details: Used when resolution is impossible or disputed.

buy(uint256,bool,uint256).notice: Buy YES or NO outcome tokens for a market.

closeMarket(uint256).notice: Close a market after its end time (required before resolution).

createMarket(string,uint256,address).notice: Create a new binary outcome market.

impliedProbabilityYES(uint256).notice: Implied probability of YES winning, in BPS (5000 = 50%)

marketCreators(address).notice: Secondary allowlist of addresses authorised to call createMarket         in addition to the admin. Used on devnet/integration runs so the         QA Bot's `verify-onchain-integration.sh` can drive the full         create→buy→resolve flow from a single tester key without         transferring admin away from the deployer. On production         networks this mapping is expected to remain empty (or curated)         and the contract behaves as if it were single-admin.

resolve(uint256,bool).notice: Resolve a market as YES or NO.

setMarketCreator(address,bool).notice: Grant or revoke market-creation rights for an address. Admin-only.

voidMarket(uint256).notice: Void a market (return collateral 1:1 to token holders).

documentation.text:
@title MarketFactory
 @notice Creates and resolves GoodPredict binary outcome markets.
         Flow:
           1. Admin creates a market with a question and resolution deadline.
           2. Anyone can buy YES/NO tokens by depositing G$ (1:1 collateral).
              Each purchase mints both YES and NO tokens to a pool; caller
              receives the side they want.
           3. After deadline, a resolver (oracle/admin) calls resolve(YES/NO).
           4. Winners redeem YES (if YES wins) or NO (if NO wins) tokens for G$.
              A 1% fee is charged on winnings and routed to the UBI fee splitter.

@notice Secondary allowlist of addresses authorised to call createMarket
         in addition to the admin. Used on devnet/integration runs so the
         QA Bot's `verify-onchain-integration.sh` can drive the full
         create→buy→resolve flow from a single tester key without
         transferring admin away from the deployer. On production
         networks this mapping is expected to remain empty (or curated)
         and the contract behaves as if it were single-admin.

@dev Admin OR any address explicitly granted via `setMarketCreator`.
      Reverts with the same `NotAdmin()` selector to keep the existing
      revert ABI stable for tooling that already pattern-matches on
      `0x7bfa4b9f`.
